rulings.Case Title: SANDRA SHEWRY VS. LYUDMILA PASTERNAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination & Reduction Of Lien Pursuant To Welfare & Institution Code Section 14124.76
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 12, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS LYDUMILA PASTERNAK, ALBERT STOLL JR. Motion For Determination & Reduction Of Lien Pursuant To Welfare & Institution Code Section 14124.76. Motion for determination and reduction of lien denied as to request to reduce lien amount to zero. Fitch vs. Select Products Company (2005) 36 Cal.4th 812 is not applicable because this is not solely a wrongful death action. Defendants have not met the elements of estoppel, and no showing that this case meets the heightened standard for estoppel against a public agency. Court uses the Ahlborn (2006) 547 U.S. 268 formule reference in W&I Code section 14124.76 based upon a $250,000.00 claim. Counsel to meet and confer regarding the precise amount owed using the Ahlborn analysis with the appropriate deductions. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA COUNTY SUPERINTENDENTS EDUCATIONAL VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION ON THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney Fees; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Index Of Non Calif. Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 12, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, KENNETH MARZION, LORI MCGARTLAND, SHAREN SCOTT Motion For Attorney Fees. Motion for attorney fees granted. The claims against the individual defendants were dismissed with prejudice in federal court two months before this state action was filed. Defendants prevailed in this Court on the 42 U.S.C. section 1983 claims when they were dismissed with prejudice as res judicata. The claims brought in state Court were meritless and without foundation. The court uses the lodestar analysis in awarding defendants reasonable and necessary fees in the amount of $36,051. The court disallows fees for the anti-Slapp and punitive damages analysis, and for a portion of the fees attributed to this fee motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA COUNTY SUPERINTENDENTS EDUCATIONAL VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION ON THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended)Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 12, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC, KENNETH MARZION, LORI MCGARTLAND, SHAREN SCOTT (Amended) Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion granted. The first cause of action fails to state a claim that the regulations and administrative framework are unconstitional as applied. Writ review is available post-hearing. Plaintiffs fail to show how the totality of the administrative rights available to plaintiff does not comport with due process. The first cause of action also fails to state a claim that the regulations are facially unconstitutional. A hearing must be held within a "reasonable" time when not fixed by statute, and plaintiffs have a right to immediate writ review where a hearing is not held within a reasonable time. No exhaustion of remedies demonstrated. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE YOSHI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Memorandum Ofp/A In Support Thereof;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 12, 2010, line 14, PETITIONER EUGENE YOSHI Motion For Writ Of Mandate. Application for Writ of Mandate denied. Petitioner has not yet exhausted administrative remedies. Respondent has not issued a final decision and retains jurisdiction over this matter. Government Code section does not apply to Petitioner who is a City employee. No due process violation because petitioner fails to show prejudice. =(302/CWW)
